messages.content:
Choose the answer with the word which CANNOT fill in the gap.
I'm going to my English class and I'm going to the bar ...
O afterwards
O later
O after
O soon
O afterwards

Choose the answer with the meaning that best matches the sentence below. James is upset now as he failed his admission to university.
• If he didn't fail the exam, he
wouldn't be upset now
• If he hadn't failed the exam, he wouldn't have been upset now
• If he hadn't failed the exam, he
wouldn't be upset now
© If he didn't fail the exam, he wouldn't have been upset now
© If he didn't fail the exam, he wouldn't be upset now

the stranger entered the town, he met by a soldier who asked, "Are you a traveler?" "So it would appear,' he answered carelessly.
• As the stranger entered the town, he met by a soldier who asked if he was a traveler. He answered carelessly that it would appear
• As the stranger entered the town, he had met by a soldier who had asked if he had been a traveler. He had carelessly answered that it would have been appear
• As the stranger entered the town, he met by a soldier who was asked if he were a traveler.
He answered carelessly that it would appear
• As the stranger entered the town, he met by a soldier who was asking to be a traveler. He answered that it would appear
• As the stranger entered the town, he had met by a soldier who had asked if he had been a traveler. He had carelessly answered that it would have been appear

44. Choose the correct answer to complete the sentence. I have very strict teachers and they won't allow me ... the classroom if I'm late.
O enter
O entering
O to enter
O entered
45. Choose the

correct answer to complete the sentence. I have very strict teachers and they won't allow me ... the classroom if I'm late.
O enter
O entering
O to enter
O entered

The correct answer is "to enter."

45. Choose the most appropriate answer to complete the
conversation. ..? Can you speak up?
I can't hear you very well.
O who is he?
• Who it is?
O who's is?
• who is it?
The most appropriate answer to complete the conversation would be "Who is it?"

46. Choose the correct answer to complete the sentence below. And ... to wrap her own presents the night before Christmas in order to save your face in front of her parents.
O under no circumstances will you
ask her
O for no circumstances you will ask
her
O under no circumstances you will
ask her
O for no circumstances should you
ask her
The correct answer to complete the sentence is "under no circumstances should you ask her."

Choose the correct answer to complete the sentence below.
Visitors ... kindly... to turn off their phones in the waiting area.
• are/asked
O won't/ asked
O are to /ask
O were/had asked
The correct answer to complete the sentence is "are asked."

48. Choose the correct answer to complete the sentence below. It is recommended that ... if he wants to hike to the bottom of the Grand
Canyon.
• he will take a gallon of water
with him
• he takes a gallon of water with
him
O he took a gallon of water with
him
• he take a gallon of water with
The correct answer to complete the sentence is "he take a gallon of water with him."

Choose the correct answer to complete the sentence below. If you'd mentioned this earlier, it ... necessary to call the doctor.
O might not have been
O could be
O would have been being © should had been
The correct answer to complete the sentence is "might not have been."

Choose the sentence with ONE grammatical mistake. (1) By the time
Tom noticed the doorbell, it already rang three times. (2) As usual, he had been listening to loud music on his stereo. (3) He turned the stereo down and stood up to answer the door. (4) An old man was standing on the steps.
01
02
03
04
Testni yakunlash

The sentence with ONE grammatical mistake is:

(1) By the time Tom noticed the doorbell, it already rang three times.

The correct version should be:

By the time Tom noticed the doorbell, it had already rung three times.

The grammatical mistake is the use of "rang" instead of "had already rung."
